Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.model.board

Class Board

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.model.board.Board
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Board extends Object implements Serializable
A 10x10 Battleship board: the grid of Cells, the fleet placed on it, and the rules for placing ships and resolving shots.

A Board is serializable on its own, satisfying HU-5's requirement to save the board's state to a file and restore it later. BoardListeners are intentionally not part of that saved state (see listeners); after loading a saved board, the controller is expected to re-attach its listener before using it.

See Also:

    private final Cell[][]
    cells

    listeners
    Listeners are UI/controller wiring, not game state, so they are excluded from serialization and re-created empty on every load (see readObject(java.io.ObjectInputStream)).
    private static final long
    serialVersionUID
     
    private final List<Ship>
    ships
     
    static final int

    Both dimensions of the board: 10x10.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Board()
    Creates an empty 10x10 board: every cell starts as CellState.EMPTY.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addListener(BoardListener listener)
     
    boolean
    areAllShipsSunk()
     
    boolean
    canBeShotAt(Position position)
     
    boolean
    canPlaceShip(Ship ship)
    Checks whether ship could be placed on this board right now, without actually placing it and without throwing on failure.
    private boolean
    fitsWithinBoard(Ship ship)
    Whether every cell ship would occupy is within the board's 0-9 range, using Ship.getWidthInColumns() and Ship.getDepthInRows() as the single source of truth for the ship's footprint -- deliberately computed with plain arithmetic rather than by calling Ship.getOccupiedPositions(): that method builds a Position for every segment, and Position's own constructor rejects out-of-range coordinates -- exactly the case being checked for here, so it can't be used to check for it.
    Cell
    getCell(Position position)
     
    List<Ship>
    getShips()
     
    private boolean
    overlapsExistingShip(Ship ship)
     
    void
    placeShip(Ship ship)
    Places a ship on the board, after validating that it fits within the board and doesn't overlap any ship already placed.
    private void
    readObject(ObjectInputStream inputStream)
    Restores the transient listeners list after deserialization -- it is never part of the saved state, so it is always recreated empty; the controller re-attaches itself after loading.
    ShotResult
    receiveShot(Position position)
    Resolves a shot at the given position: marks the cell, updates the hit ship (if any), and notifies listeners.
    void
    removeListener(BoardListener listener)
     
    private void
    validateWithinBounds(Ship ship)

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID

    • SIZE

      public static final int SIZE
      Both dimensions of the board: 10x10.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • cells

      private final Cell[][] cells

    • ships

      private final List<Ship> ships

    • listeners

      private transient List<BoardListener> listeners
      Listeners are UI/controller wiring, not game state, so they are excluded from serialization and re-created empty on every load (see readObject(java.io.ObjectInputStream)).

  • Constructor Details

    • Board

      public Board()
      Creates an empty 10x10 board: every cell starts as CellState.EMPTY.

  • Method Details

    • placeShip

      public void placeShip(Ship ship) throws InvalidShipPlacementException
      Places a ship on the board, after validating that it fits within the board and doesn't overlap any ship already placed.
      Parameters:
      ship - the ship to place
      Throws:
      InvalidShipPlacementException - if the ship would run off the edge of the board or overlap an existing ship

    • canPlaceShip

      public boolean canPlaceShip(Ship ship)
      Checks whether ship could be placed on this board right now, without actually placing it and without throwing on failure. Meant for live placement previews, so the player can be shown whether their current cursor position is valid before they click.
      Parameters:
      ship - a ship that has not been placed anywhere yet
      Returns:
      true if placeShip(com.example.battleship.model.ship.Ship) would currently succeed for this exact ship

    • validateWithinBounds

      private void validateWithinBounds(Ship ship) throws OutOfBoardException
      Throws:
      OutOfBoardException

    • fitsWithinBoard

      private boolean fitsWithinBoard(Ship ship)
      Whether every cell ship would occupy is within the board's 0-9 range, using Ship.getWidthInColumns() and Ship.getDepthInRows() as the single source of truth for the ship's footprint -- deliberately computed with plain arithmetic rather than by calling Ship.getOccupiedPositions(): that method builds a Position for every segment, and Position's own constructor rejects out-of-range coordinates -- exactly the case being checked for here, so it can't be used to check for it.

    • overlapsExistingShip

      private boolean overlapsExistingShip(Ship ship)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - indirectly if ship doesn't fit -- always check fitsWithinBoard(com.example.battleship.model.ship.Ship) first.

    • receiveShot

      public ShotResult receiveShot(Position position)
      Resolves a shot at the given position: marks the cell, updates the hit ship (if any), and notifies listeners.
      Parameters:
      position - the cell being shot at
      Returns:
      the outcome of the shot
      Throws:
      CellAlreadyShotException - if this cell has already been shot at

    • canBeShotAt

      public boolean canBeShotAt(Position position)
      Parameters:
      position - a board position
      Returns:
      true if that cell has not been shot at yet

    • areAllShipsSunk

      public boolean areAllShipsSunk()
      Returns:
      true once every ship placed on this board has been sunk

    • getCell

      public Cell getCell(Position position)
      Parameters:
      position - a board position
      Returns:
      the cell at that position

    • getShips

      public List<Ship> getShips()
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of every ship placed on this board

    • addListener

      public void addListener(BoardListener listener)

    • removeListener

      public void removeListener(BoardListener listener)

    • readObject

      private void readObject(ObjectInputStream inputStream) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Restores the transient listeners list after deserialization -- it is never part of the saved state, so it is always recreated empty; the controller re-attaches itself after loading.
      Throws:
      IOException
      ClassNotFoundException
